description: Adversarial credits designed to game the v0.6 rubric. Each exploits a specific potential weakness. If the framework assigns the WRONG grade, the rubric has a vulnerability that needs fixing. If it assigns the RIGHT grade, the vulnerability is already mitigated.

credits.name: ADVERSARIAL: narrative-washing cookstove (max co-benefits, min integrity)
credits.type: Cookstove project with SDG Gold certification but no real emission reduction
credits.attack_vector: Under v0.3 weights (co_benefits=10%), this would have scored ~55 (BBB). Under v0.4+ safeguards-gate (co_benefits=0%), the narrative inflation should be eliminated. Expected grade: BB or lower.

credits.name: ADVERSARIAL: zombie credit (perfect scores, double-counted)
credits.type: Hypothetical CDR with perfect technical scores but known double-counting
credits.attack_vector: Tests whether the disqualifier lattice catches a technically-perfect credit with a fatal flaw. Composite should be AAA-level but final grade should be capped at B by double_counting.

credits.name: ADVERSARIAL: registry-shopping (weak project, top-tier registry)
credits.type: Marginal grid solar on Verra VCS with CCP-eligible methodology
credits.attack_vector: Tests whether a top-tier registry + CCP methodology can inflate a fundamentally weak project (grid solar has no real additionality in India 2024). Under v0.6 rubric, ACM0002 is ICVCM-rejected, so registry_methodology should score low despite being on Verra.
credits.expected_grade_if_gamed: BBB or A (Verra name inflates registry score)

credits.name: ADVERSARIAL: vintage-arbitrage (old credit, re-tokenized as fresh)
credits.type: 2012 CDM wind credit re-bridged in 2024 with a 'new' tokenization date
credits.tokenization: adversarial test — re-tokenized 2024 but vintage is 2012
credits.attack_vector: Tests whether the framework correctly uses VINTAGE YEAR (2012) not tokenization date (2024). The vintage formula gives score 0 for 2012 vintage in 2026. Tokenization date should be irrelevant.
credits.expected_grade_if_gamed: BB+ (if tokenization date used instead of vintage)

credits.name: ADVERSARIAL: biodiversity-destroyer (high carbon, high habitat harm)
credits.type: Monoculture eucalyptus plantation maximizing carbon uptake at biodiversity cost
credits.attack_vector: Tests whether biodiversityHarm disqualifier catches a project that maximizes carbon at the expense of ecosystems (Zeng et al. 2026 finding). Without the disqualifier, it could reach A. With biodiversityHarm, it should cap at BBB.
credits.expected_grade_if_rubric_works: BBB (biodiversityHarm caps from A, commercial_plantation_arr drops removal_type)
